item.title: Version 3.2.1
item.description:
Patch - Version 3.2.1

Friendlier error message when the Ollama Thinking toggle (introduced in v3.2) is set to On for a model that doesn't support reasoning.

  • Previously: a truncated raw HTTP 400 banner reading Ollama returned HTTP 400: {"error":...}
  • Now: llama3.2:3b does not support thinking mode with a recovery hint pointing back at the toggle (Set Thinking to Auto or Off in Performance options, then run again.).
  • The benchmark error banner now wraps long messages and shows the recovery suggestion as a secondary line, so future error paths surface their fix-it hint automatically.

item.title: Version 3.2
item.description:
Highlights - Version 3.2

  • Ollama thinking-mode toggle. A new tri-state Picker (Auto / On / Off) under the benchmark Performance disclosure controls Ollama's think request parameter. Auto omits the field so the model uses its server-side default (the safe choice for older Ollama versions or non-thinking models). Set On to force reasoning, Off to force-disable it.
  • Auto-update feed moved to GitHub Pages. v3.2+ pulls the appcast from uncsoft.github.io/anubis-oss/appcast.xml for full version-controlled history of every release notice. Older v3.0/v3.1 installs continue to work via the existing devpadapp.com URL.

Builds on the v3.1 reasoning-aware metrics work (see release notes for v3.1 below).

item.title: Version 3.1
item.description:
Highlights - Version 3.1

Reasoning-aware metrics, prefill speed, accurate TTFT.

  • Output tokens/sec now excludes thinking time. Reasoning models like DeepSeek-R1, Qwen3-thinking, GLM, and gpt-oss stream their thoughts on a separate channel; previously this time was wrongly charged against TTFT and the tokens were counted as output. Anubis now decodes reasoning content and surfaces it as <think>...</think> in the response.
  • Prefill (input) tokens/sec is now a first-class metric. Visible on the TTFT card, in session history, in CSV export, and on the leaderboard.
  • Reasoning split. Thinking-model runs record reasoning tokens and reasoning duration separately; the session detail view shows reasoning tok/s alongside output tok/s.
  • Better error messages for backend failures - Ollama HTTP errors no longer surface as "timed out after 0 seconds."
  • Leaderboard explorer gains Prefill tok/s and Reasoning tok/s columns.

Fixes #17 and #18.

item.title: Version 3.0
item.description:
Highlights - Version 3.0

  • Apple Intelligence backend - on-device Foundation Models (macOS 26+).
  • Reports - Export - Markdown or CSV of the per-model summary.
  • Monitor redesign - framed empty state, big Start button, stress menus from idle.
  • Benchmark UI density pass - 3-column charts, collapsible Session Details, trailing Run Time card, cleaner x-axis.
  • Streaming text fix - disabled kerning/ligatures to drop CoreText pair-positioning from the layout hot path.

What's New in 2.9

Hardware Stress Testing

Push your Apple Silicon to its limits from within the Monitor:

  • CPU stress - spawns processes per core (All Cores, P-Cores, E-Cores, or Single Core)
  • GPU stress - Metal Mandelbrot fractal zoom with 4 intensity levels
  • Memory bandwidth stress - continuous streaming with measured GB/s throughput
  • Safety: 5-minute auto-timeout, thermal watchdog, GPU auto-downgrade

Floating Monitor HUD

Compact, always-on-top system metrics overlay - launchable from any tab. Live CPU, GPU, memory, power, frequency, and thermal state on a dark glass widget.

New Benchmark Prompts

5 new built-in prompts: Causal Reasoning, System Design, Dialogue Scene, Historical Analysis, Constrained Writing (15 total).

Other Improvements

  • System-wide memory tracking (active + wired + compressed)
  • Backend process memory overlay on memory chart

item.title: Version 2.9.0
item.description:
What's New in 2.9

Hardware Stress Testing

Push your Apple Silicon to its limits from within the Monitor:

  • CPU stress - spawns processes per core (All Cores, P-Cores, E-Cores, or Single Core)
  • GPU stress - Metal Mandelbrot fractal zoom with 4 intensity levels
  • Memory bandwidth stress - continuous streaming with measured GB/s throughput
  • Safety: 5-minute auto-timeout, thermal watchdog, GPU auto-downgrade

Floating Monitor HUD

Compact, always-on-top system metrics overlay - launchable from any tab. Live CPU, GPU, memory, power, frequency, and thermal state on a dark glass widget.

New Benchmark Prompts

5 new built-in prompts: Causal Reasoning, System Design, Dialogue Scene, Historical Analysis, Constrained Writing (15 total).

Other Improvements

  • System-wide memory tracking (active + wired + compressed)
  • Backend process memory overlay on memory chart

item.title: Version 2.8.0
item.description:
What's New in v2.8

System Monitor

  • New standalone system monitor mode - live CPU, GPU, memory, power, and thermal charts without running a benchmark
  • Adaptive chart sizing that fits all rows in a single window

Tok/s Accuracy Fix

  • Fixed eval duration for OpenAI-compatible backends (LM Studio, vLLM, etc.) - no longer includes model load + prompt prefill time
  • Parses OpenAI usage object for accurate token counts

Other Improvements

  • Model quantization and GGUF/MLX format tracking
  • Pull Model button in benchmark toolbar
  • 3-column expanded dashboard layout
